title: Hattie the Mouse and the Massive Shopping List
summary: Hattie the Mouse runs a specialized hat workshop inside the Hollow Tree, where she crafts unique headwear for her neighbors. Her day begins with a visit from Mischief Mouse, who orders a multi-colored hat that Hattie surprisingly finishes by baking it in her oven. The quiet day is quickly interrupted by Victor Mouse, who arrives with a massive wish list including everything from mittens and sneakers to thermometers and bedroom curtains. Realizing she only makes hats, Hattie kindly offers to close her shop and drive Victor to a local store so he can fulfill his enormous shopping list.

highlightQuote.text: Victor said, 'I would like no hats, but I would like a coat, and hats, and little mittens, and little sneakers and glasses and boots and sandals and I would like some pajamas and a blanket and I would like some paintings on my bedroom...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want a story about a hat... about a hat boutique.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a story about Hattie Mouse making a hat in her hat workshop?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now it's time for our stars and story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I have to do so much exercise.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need a story about Hattie Mouse making hats for everybody in the neighborhood and even Lily and Snake and everyone else.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the Mouse was sitting in her workshop inside the Hollow Tree. And someone was knocking at the door.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's about her making hats.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, yeah, we'll get there. And Hattie the Mouse said, 'Oh, coming!' and then she said, 'Who is it?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse is there. No, that's Mischief Mouse coming.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse said, 'Oh, hello Hattie, it's me, Mischief Mouse.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about a green, brown, brown, tan and white hat? And some cardboard too, so I got it myself.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie the Mouse said, 'Oh, a green and brown and white and tan hat. I think I have some cardboard around here and some colored paints.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse said, 'Well, thank you very much. I'll wait outside. Maybe I'll swing on that swing.' Because Hattie the Mouse had a nice swing hanging from a branch just outside her house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, and then the hat was going to be baked.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she said, 'The hat is almost done, I'm just baking it right now.' And it was in the oven... Ding! It was all done.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She put the hat on and then, 'Hmm, this hat is perfect.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did someone else come to Hattie Mouse's workshop to get a hat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Victor Mouse showed up. He knocked on the door and Hattie let him in. What did Victor say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Victor said, 'I would like no hats, but I would like a coat, and hats, and little mittens, and little sneakers and glasses and boots and sandals and I would like some pajamas and a blanket and I would like some paintings on my bedroom and I would like some windows and curtains and I would like some crayons and pictures on my room and a thermometer and some stars up there and a lamp with a chair and then I would like a shirt too and I would like some socks and I would like pants and I would like a shirt and I would like some little buttons and a little belt and Hattie, Victor wanted flowers and tea too.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie the Mouse said, 'Wow, Victor. That's a lot of things. I can take you shopping for all the rest of those things. Let's go together.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie the Mouse and Victor got in the car together to go to the store. And that's the end of the story for now.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Victor Mouse
characters.description: A daring racer who pilots a vibrant yellow and blue bananamobile through the muddiest shortcuts, this mouse is fueled by a fastidious and singular ambition. Every victory brings him closer to fulfilling an incredibly long, exacting manifest of acquisitions, ranging from tailored velvet waistcoats to mahogany bureaus and gilded parlor trim.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

characters.name: Snake
characters.description: Another neighbor in the community served by Hattie's hat boutique.

places.name: The Hollow Tree
places.description: The location of Hattie's cozy workshop where she crafts and bakes her hats.

places.name: The Neighborhood
places.description: The surrounding community where all the mice and animal friends live.

places.name: The Store
places.description: A versatile apothecary and provisioner where Seeker procures heavy-lidded sleep potions and Hattie guides Victor toward his exhaustive list of non-hat essentials. The eclectic inventory doubles as a grocery for all sizes, where local mice browse for carrots alongside a larder of gluten-free broccoli prized for its refreshing silence. Tucked among these oddities are the Band-Aids that once stirred a flicker of temporary concern in Izzy.
